---
title: "BIS Speeches – Data Exploration and Inflation Persistence Flags (Q3)"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    number_sections: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
dir.create("figures", showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create("tables",  showWarnings = FALSE)
```

# Libraries

```{r libraries}
library(readr)      # fast CSV reading with type guessing
library(dplyr)      # data manipulation
library(lubridate)  # date parsing
library(stringr)    # text operations and word boundary counts
library(ggplot2)    # plotting
```

# Load Data

```{r load_data}
# Read BIS speech corpus; higher guess_max improves column type detection
# on large text files.
speeches <- read_csv("BIS_speeches.csv", guess_max = 100000)

# Retain only the columns needed for this analysis
speeches <- speeches %>%
  select(url, title, description, date, text, author)
```

# Clean

```{r clean}
speeches <- speeches %>%
  mutate(
    date_parsed = lubridate::ymd(date, quiet = TRUE),
    year        = lubridate::year(date_parsed),
    author      = stringr::str_squish(as.character(author)),
    title       = stringr::str_squish(as.character(title)),
    text        = as.character(text)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(date_parsed), !is.na(text)) %>%
  distinct(url, date_parsed, author, title, .keep_all = TRUE)
```

# Word Count

```{r word_count}
# Consistent speech-length metric using linguistic word boundaries
speeches <- speeches %>%
  mutate(word_count = str_count(text, boundary("word")))
```

# Part A: Basic Corpus Statistics

```{r summary_stats}
total_speeches   <- nrow(speeches)
time_start       <- min(speeches$date_parsed, na.rm = TRUE)
time_end         <- max(speeches$date_parsed, na.rm = TRUE)
unique_speakers  <- n_distinct(speeches$author)

wc                <- speeches$word_count
word_count_min    <- min(wc,    na.rm = TRUE)
word_count_max    <- max(wc,    na.rm = TRUE)
word_count_mean   <- mean(wc,   na.rm = TRUE)
word_count_median <- median(wc, na.rm = TRUE)
word_count_sd     <- sd(wc,     na.rm = TRUE)

# Additional feature: flag speeches that mention "inflation"
speeches <- speeches %>%
  mutate(mentions_inflation =
           str_detect(text, regex("\\binflation\\b", ignore_case = TRUE)))

share_mention_inflation <- mean(speeches$mentions_inflation, na.rm = TRUE)

# Summary table
basic_exploration <- tibble::tibble(
  total_speeches,
  time_start              = as.Date(time_start),
  time_end                = as.Date(time_end),
  unique_speakers,
  word_count_min,
  word_count_max,
  word_count_mean,
  word_count_median,
  word_count_sd,
  share_mention_inflation
)

readr::write_csv(basic_exploration, "tables/basic_exploration.csv")
basic_exploration
```

```{r speeches_per_year}
per_year <- speeches %>%
  count(year, name = "n_speeches") %>%
  arrange(year)

ggplot(per_year, aes(year, n_speeches)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    title = "BIS Speeches per Year",
    x     = "Year",
    y     = "Number of speeches"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

ggsave("figures/speeches_per_year.png", width = 8, height = 4.5, dpi = 200)
```

# Part B: Country Assignment

```{r country_map}
# Lookup table mapping institution keywords to country labels.
# Matching is done on a concatenation of the description and author fields.
country_map <- c(
  "federal reserve"             = "United States",
  "board of governors"          = "United States",
  "bank of england"             = "United Kingdom",
  "european central bank"       = "Euro area",
  "ecb"                         = "Euro area",
  "deutsche bundesbank"         = "Germany",
  "banque de france"            = "France",
  "banca d'italia"              = "Italy",
  "bank of japan"               = "Japan",
  "people's bank of china"      = "China",
  "bank of canada"              = "Canada",
  "banco de mexico"             = "Mexico",
  "banco central do brasil"     = "Brazil",
  "sveriges riksbank"           = "Sweden",
  "bank of sweden"              = "Sweden",
  "reserve bank of australia"   = "Australia",
  "reserve bank of new zealand" = "New Zealand"
)
```

```{r assign_country}
assign_country <- function(description, author) {
  text <- tolower(paste(description, author))
  for (key in names(country_map)) {
    if (grepl(key, text, fixed = TRUE)) {
      return(country_map[[key]])
    }
  }
  return("Unknown")
}

speeches <- speeches %>%
  mutate(country = mapply(assign_country, description, author))
```

```{r top10_countries}
top10 <- speeches %>%
  filter(country != "Unknown") %>%
  count(country, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)

top10
readr::write_csv(top10, "tables/top10_countries.csv")
```

```{r speeches_by_country_year}
selected_countries <- c("United States", "United Kingdom", "Euro area")

per_year_country <- speeches %>%
  filter(country %in% selected_countries) %>%
  count(year, country)

ggplot(per_year_country, aes(x = year, y = n, color = country)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    title = "Speeches per Year – Selected Central Banks",
    x     = "Year",
    y     = "Number of speeches"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

ggsave("figures/speeches_per_year_top3_countries.png", width = 8, height = 4.5, dpi = 200)
```

# Part C: Inflation Persistence Flags

```{r persistence_patterns}
# A curated set of phrases that capture language indicating persistent inflation.
# Kept deliberately narrow to minimize false positives — the goal is to identify
# speeches where the speaker explicitly characterizes inflation as persistent,
# not merely mentions it.
persist_patterns <- c(
  "persistent inflation",
  "inflation remains high",
  "inflation remains elevated",
  "inflation remains above target",
  "sticky inflation",
  "underlying inflation remains",
  "price pressures.*(persist|continue|expected to continue|likely to continue)",
  "inflation pressures.*(persist|continue|expected to continue|likely to continue)",
  "persistence of inflation",
  "second[- ]round effects"
)

persist_regex <- paste(persist_patterns, collapse = "|")
```

```{r flag_speeches}
speeches <- speeches %>%
  mutate(flag_persist = str_detect(text, regex(persist_regex, ignore_case = TRUE)))
```

```{r flag_validation}
# Manual check: inspect flagged speeches to confirm the pattern is working as intended
speeches %>%
  filter(flag_persist) %>%
  select(date_parsed, author, title) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)
```

```{r flag_summary}
n_flagged <- sum(speeches$flag_persist, na.rm = TRUE)
n_total   <- nrow(speeches)
share_all <- n_flagged / n_total

tibble::tibble(
  total_speeches      = n_total,
  flagged_persistence = n_flagged,
  share_flagged       = share_all
)
```

```{r persistence_by_year}
per_year_persist <- speeches %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarize(
    total   = n(),
    flagged = sum(flag_persist, na.rm = TRUE),
    share   = ifelse(total > 0, flagged / total, NA_real_),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(year)

per_year_persist
readr::write_csv(per_year_persist, "tables/share_persistence_by_year.csv")
```

```{r persistence_plot}
ggplot(per_year_persist, aes(x = year, y = share)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Share of Speeches Mentioning Inflation Persistence by Year",
    x     = "Year",
    y     = "Share of speeches"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

ggsave("figures/share_inflation_persistence_by_year.png", width = 8, height = 4.5, dpi = 200)
```
